library.name: Basic Writing Frames
library.description: Foundational writing-oriented sentence frames for giving opinions, organizing ideas, adding examples, and making clear academic-style statements.

items.title: I think that...
items.pattern: I think (that) + clause
items.brief: Use it to state a direct personal opinion in a simple and natural way.
contrastHints: in my opinion / from my point of view
it seems that / it appears that

items.title: in my opinion / from my point of view
items.pattern: In my opinion, ... / From my point of view, ...
items.brief: Use it to mark a personal opinion more explicitly and in a more writing-friendly way.

items.title: it seems that / it appears that
items.pattern: it seems that... / it appears that...
items.brief: Use it to make a softer judgment when you do not want to sound too absolute.

items.title: according to...
items.pattern: according to + source
items.brief: Use it to introduce information from a source instead of presenting it as your own idea.

items.title: for example / for instance / such as
items.pattern: for example / for instance / such as
items.brief: Use it to add examples, while choosing correctly between sentence-level and phrase-level example markers.
contrastHints: to begin with / first of all

items.title: for one thing... for another...
items.pattern: for one thing..., for another...
items.brief: Use it to present two separate reasons or considerations in an organized way.
contrastHints: first of all / to begin with
on the one hand / on the other hand

items.title: first of all / to begin with
items.pattern: first of all, ... / to begin with, ...
items.brief: Use it to open a list or the first major point in a clear and organized way.

items.title: in addition / moreover / besides
items.pattern: in addition / moreover / besides
items.brief: Use it to add another point, while choosing the right tone from neutral to more formal or more conversational.
contrastHints: first of all / to begin with
for example / for instance / such as

items.title: finally / in conclusion / to sum up
items.pattern: finally / in conclusion / to sum up
items.brief: Use it to close a paragraph or short essay with the right level of directness and formality.
contrastHints: first of all / to begin with

items.title: on the one hand... on the other hand...
items.pattern: on the one hand..., on the other hand...
items.brief: Use it to present two sides of an issue in a balanced and organized way.

items.title: as a result, ...
items.pattern: as a result, statement
items.brief: Use it to state the result of what has just been discussed in a clear writing-oriented way.
contrastHints: for example / for instance / such as

items.title: the fact is that...
items.pattern: the fact is that + clause
items.brief: Use it to state what you see as the central reality or key point in an argument.
contrastHints: it seems that / it appears that

items.title: there is no doubt that...
items.pattern: there is no doubt that + clause
items.brief: Use it when you want to make a very strong, confident claim in a formal style.
contrastHints: it seems that / it appears that

items.title: it is important to / it is necessary to
items.pattern: it is important to... / it is necessary to...
items.brief: Use it to state importance or necessity in a neutral, useful writing style.
contrastHints: it is helpful to / it is useful to

items.title: it is helpful to / it is useful to
items.pattern: it is helpful to... / it is useful to...
items.brief: Use it to recommend a practical action without sounding as strong as necessary or important.
contrastHints: it is important to / it is necessary to

items.title: it is hard to / it is easy to
items.pattern: it is hard to... / it is easy to...
items.brief: Use it to judge how easy or difficult an action is in a clean, objective way.
contrastHints: it is important to / it is necessary to

items.title: it is common for... to...
items.pattern: it is common for + noun + to + base
items.brief: Use it to describe a general pattern or typical behavior in a more objective style.
contrastHints: it seems that / it appears that

items.title: one of the most ... is ...
items.pattern: one of the most + adjective + nouns is...
items.brief: Use it to present an item as a highly important example within a larger group.

items.title: the best way to... is to...
items.pattern: the best way to + goal + is to + action
items.brief: Use it to propose a clear solution or method in a direct and useful format.
contrastHints: it is important to / it is necessary to

items.title: what matters is...
items.pattern: what matters is + noun / clause
items.brief: Use it to shift attention to the most important point in a discussion.
contrastHints: the best way to... is to...

items.title: one reason is that... another reason is that...
items.pattern: one reason is that... another reason is that...
items.brief: Use it to list multiple reasons in a very explicit, beginner-friendly writing structure.

items.title: for this reason, ...
items.pattern: for this reason, statement
items.brief: Use it to connect a previous explanation to a following conclusion in a formal but clear way.

items.title: this shows that... / this means that...
items.pattern: this shows that... / this means that...
items.brief: Use it to explain what an example, fact, or result proves or suggests.
contrastHints: it seems that / it appears that

items.title: it is worth noting that...
items.pattern: it is worth noting that + clause
items.brief: Use it to draw attention to an important supporting point in a formal, academic tone.
